title: Smoky Gooey Portuguese Chickpeas Stir-fry
description: A romantic vegan-friendly dish featuring smoky flavors and a gooey texture from the combination of chickpeas with ground cumin.
instructions: Rinse the garbanzo beans and soak them in water for at least 1 hour.
Drain the soaked beans, rinse again with fresh water, and transfer to a pot. Add enough water to cover the beans by about an inch.
Bring the beans to a boil over medium heat, then reduce heat to low, partially cover, and simmer until tender but not falling apart (about 30 minutes).
While beans are cooking, finely chop onion and mince garlic cloves.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet or wok. Add chopped onion and sauté until soft and translucent. Stir in minced garlic and cook for about 1 minute.
Stir in tomato paste, ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, black pepper, and cooked beans from the pot. Bring to a simmer.
Add pitted green olives on top of the bean mixture and let them absorb the flavors (about 5 minutes).
Serve hot as a side dish or main course.
